value.testSuite: User Registration and Account Deletion Suite
value.description: Covers new user signup flow including account creation, login verification, and account deletion on automationexercise.com

tests.name: Happy Path: New user signup, account creation, and deletion

steps.description: Launch browser and navigate to home page
Click on Signup / Login button in navbar
Verify New User Signup! heading is visible
Enter email address in signup form
Verify ENTER ACCOUNT INFORMATION section is visible

tests.name: Happy Path: Complete account information, create account, verify login, and delete account

steps.description: Select checkbox Sign up for our newsletter!
Select checkbox Receive special offers from our partners!
Verify ACCOUNT CREATED! message is visible
Click Continue button after account creation
Verify Logged in as username is visible in navbar
Verify ACCOUNT DELETED! message is visible
Click Continue button after account deletion
Capture final state after account deletion flow

tests.name: Negative: Signup with already registered email

steps.description: Verify New User Signup! heading is visible
Enter name of an already registered user
Verify error message for duplicate email is shown
Capture error state for duplicate email signup attempt

value.description: Covers new user signup from home page through account creation, login verification, and account deletion on automationexercise.com

tests.name: Happy Path: New user signup, account creation, and account deletion

steps.description: Launch browser and navigate to home page
Click on Signup / Login button in the navigation header
Verify New User Signup! heading is visible on the right form
Enter name in the signup form
Enter unique email address in the signup form
Click the Signup button to proceed to account information form
Verify ENTER ACCOUNT INFORMATION heading is visible
Select checkbox Sign up for our newsletter!
Select checkbox Receive special offers from our partners!
Verify ACCOUNT CREATED! message is displayed
Click Continue button after account creation
Verify user is logged in and username is displayed in the header
Verify ACCOUNT DELETED! message is displayed
Click Continue button after account deletion
Capture final state after account deletion flow completes

tests.name: Negative: Signup attempt with already registered email

steps.description: Verify New User Signup! form is visible
Enter name for an already registered account
Enter an email address that already exists in the system
Click Signup button with duplicate email
Verify error message is shown for duplicate email address
Capture error state for duplicate email signup attempt

issues.message: Raw page.locator() calls used throughout instead of POM fixture methods, violating POM encapsulation principle
loginPage.navigate() used but loginPage is a POM fixture — verify it encapsulates the URL internally and does not require a raw URL argument
page.locator("a[href='/delete_account']") is a raw locator interaction that should be a POM method
Hardcoded file paths for screenshots ('test-results/TC-REG-001-final-state.png') may break in CI environments
expect.soft(page.url()).toContain(...) is synchronous but page.url() is not awaited — this is correct for page.url() but mixing soft and hard expects inconsistently
Steps 5 and 6 (filling account details and address) bypass POM — consider adding a fillAccountInfo() method to the registration page object
test.describe.configure({ mode: 'serial' }) makes the suite fragile; consider making each test independent
Inline type assertions for testData cast (as { newUser: ... }) could be replaced with a typed interface in the data file or a shared types module
TC-REG-002 lacks a homePage.navigate() call before loginPage.navigate() — starting navigation context is implicit

suggestions: Move all locator interactions (form fills, clicks, selects) into dedicated POM methods to fully comply with POM convention
Add a typed interface for test data JSON to avoid repeated inline type assertions
Consider using test.step() to wrap logical groups instead of inline comments for better Playwright HTML report readability
Add explicit waitForURL or waitForNavigation assertions after critical navigation actions (e.g., after create-account click) to avoid race conditions
Abstract screenshot calls into a utility helper to avoid hardcoded paths and ensure consistent naming across tests
